files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index fe7b639..b47ef84 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -2,24 +2,23 @@
 
 ## Strategy
-- **Survival Mode**: Cash is low ($13.7k). Burn is ~$2k/week.
-- **Capacity**: Downgraded to Tier 0 ($85/day) to save $130/day.
-- **Quality**: Increased Dev spend to $200/day to catch up with competitors (ApexSaaS, RivalTech, etc. jumped ~0.13-0.16).
-- **Quotas**: Doubled to A=10k, B=30k, C=100k to address S3/S1 complaints.
-- **Ads**: Started small S3 LinkedIn spend ($20/day).
-- **Pricing**: Plan A ($25), Plan B ($69), Plan C ($129). Removal of ongoing 15% discount caused significant churn in Week 13/14.
+- **Budget Pivot**: Massive price drop to Plan A ($15), Plan B ($49), Plan C ($89) to become the high-value budget option and offset the quality gap created by competitor jumps (~0.13-0.17).
+- **Capacity**: Upgraded to Tier 1 ($215/day) to address overload (usage > capacity) and rate-limit complaints from power users (S3).
+- **Spending**: Focusing Dev on S1 and S3 via Targeted Dev ($25/day each). Global Dev at $100. Global Ops at $100 to clear the 9-issue backlog.
+- **Ads**: Paused LinkedIn ads ($20/day) as ROI was poor (~$70/lead). Focusing on organic S1/S3.
 
 ## Metrics Tracking
-- Week 14: 118 subs ($13,782 cash).
-- Burn: ~$2k/week.
-- WTP: S1 ($35.15), S3 ($187.99).
-- ISM PMI: 46.1 (Contraction - focus on efficiency).
+- Week 15: 113 subs ($11,145 cash). 0 new subs this week.
+- Burn: Est. $3,100/week net. 
+- Runway: ~3.5 weeks.
+- WTP: S1 ($35), S3 ($188). 
+- ISM PMI: 46.1 (Contraction).
 
 ## Knowledge & Lessons
-- S1 and S3 are both sensitive to usage limits ("quota wall").
-- Competitor events significantly raise quality expectations.
-- Removing ongoing discounts has a heavy churn impact even if price < WTP.
+- Competitors (Apex, RivalTech, etc.) have reset the bar for quality (~0.35+ needed).
+- Support delays are a major source of customer friction and negative social reputation.
+- Usage/Rate limits are the primary complaint of power users (S3).
 
 ## To Do
-- Monitor S3 lead arrival from LinkedIn.
-- Check if support backlog (12 issues) clears this week.
-- Re-evaluate Plan A pricing if quality improves.
+- Monitor if $15 price point triggers S1 lead conversion.
+- Verify if support backlog clears.
+- Track S3 sentiment regarding Tier 1 infrastructure upgrade.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# 1. Infrastructure Upgrade
nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)

# 2. Pricing Adjustment
# Plan A: $15, Plan B: $49, Plan C: $89
# This is a 'Budget Leader' move to counteract the quality gap.
nm.pricing.set_prices(A=15, B=49, C=89)

# 3. Daily Spending
# Total Daily: 100 (Ops) + 100 (Dev) + 50 (Targeted Dev) = 250
# Plus Capacity T1 (215) = 465 total.
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=100, development=100)
nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S1": 25, "S3": 25})

# Stop ad spend for now to conserve cash
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={})

# 4. Social Media Update
# nm.marketing.post_social_media(content="We hear you! 🚀 We've upgraded our infrastructure (Tier 1) for better throughput and halved Plan A prices to $15/mo. We're also clearing the support backlog (9 left!). Thank you for sticking with us as we level up! #NovaMind #SaaS #BuildInPublic")

# 5. Rationale (Deprecated in favor of next-week argument)
# nm.analytics.log_rationale(...)
